THOUGHT:
The current bibliography covers ML engineering debt (Sculley et al., 2015) and distribution shift benchmarks (Koh et al., 2020). However, several key citation categories remain unaddressed, particularly "Using Specific Concepts" and "Highlighting Research Gaps" related to why models fail despite high accuracy. A foundational paper on shortcut learning and spurious correlations is critically missing, as it directly explains the mechanistic pitfalls of deep learning in real-world deployments. Adding this will allow the paper to discuss specific failure concepts, support arguments about benchmark-to-deployment gaps, and frame future research directions on robust representation learning.
